title: Add optional `unbroken` flag to CopperPour props and tests
description: Add an optional unbroken boolean to CopperPourProps to indicate that the copper pour should remain unbroken during processing or rendering.
body:
### Motivation

- Expose an optional `unbroken` boolean on copper pours so code and consumers can indicate the pour should remain unbroken when being processed or rendered.

### Description

- Add `unbroken?: boolean` to the `CopperPourProps` TypeScript interface in `lib/components/copper-pour.ts` and to the Zod schema `copperPourProps` as `z.boolean().optional()`.
- Update generated documentation files (`generated/COMPONENT_TYPES.md`, `generated/PROPS_OVERVIEW.md`) and the `README.md` to include the new `unbroken` property in the copper-pour section.
- Add a unit test `should parse unbroken` to `tests/copper-pour.test.ts` that verifies the Zod schema parses the `unbroken` flag.

### Testing

- Ran the test suite including the new test `should parse unbroken` in `tests/copper-pour.test.ts`, which verifies `copperPourProps.parse` returns `unbroken: true` for input `{ unbroken: true }`.
- All automated tests completed successfully.

------
[Codex Task](https://chatgpt.com/codex/tasks/task_b_69d59a12cf68832e80805ae1bc22655a)

title: Pass solver options (effort) into benchmark solvers and factor solver creation
description: Adds a SolverOptions type and modifies solver instantiation to allow for per-scenario solver tuning via an effort value, while maintaining existing runTask behavior.
body:
### Motivation

- Expose per-scenario solver tuning (an `effort` value) to benchmarked solver constructors so benchmarks can vary solver behavior.
- Centralize solver instantiation to make it easier to pass options and to reuse creation logic across tasks.
- Maintain existing `runTask` behavior while enabling optional solver configuration.

### Description

- Added a `SolverOptions` type and a `getBenchmarkSolverOptions` helper that reads an optional numeric `effort` field from a `SimpleRouteJson` scenario and validates it.
- Updated `getSolverConstructor` to accept solver constructors that may take an optional `SolverOptions` parameter and added `createSolverForTask` which constructs a solver with `getBenchmarkSolverOptions(task.scenario)`.
- Reworked `runTask` to use `createSolverForTask` and otherwise preserve the existing solve/measure/error handling logic.

### Testing

- Ran a TypeScript build check with `tsc --noEmit` which completed successfully.
- Ran the unit test suite with `npm test` which passed without failures.
- Performed a quick smoke run of the benchmark script (`node scripts/benchmark/benchmark-run-task.js`) to verify solver construction and solve flow, which succeeded.

------
[Codex Task](https://chatgpt.com/codex/tasks/task_b_69d59f609578832e9a3f39269b195abf)

title: chore: bump version to v0.0.405
description: Automated package update

title: Add bugreport48 fixture and Pipeline4 snapshot regression test
description: Adds a fixture and regression test for reproducing and debugging autorouting bug report 569cfe9b-1c74-4e59-b360-32ccaacfb0be, including an SVG snapshot for visualization comparison.
body:
### Motivation
- Capture and reproduce autorouting bug report `569cfe9b-1c74-4e59-b360-32ccaacfb0be` locally by adding a fixture and a deterministic regression test using Pipeline 4. 
- Make it easy to debug and prevent regressions by asserting the solver visualization with an SVG snapshot.

### Description
- Added downloaded bug report JSON and a debugger fixture at `fixtures/bug-reports/bugreport48-569cfe/bugreport48-569cfe.json` and `bugreport48-569cfe.fixture.tsx` respectively. 
- Added a Pipeline 4 regression test `tests/bugs/bugreport48-569cfe.test.ts` that uses `AutoroutingPipelineSolver4` to solve the `simple_route_json` and compares the final visualization SVG via the snapshot matcher. 
- Committed the generated SVG snapshot baseline at `tests/bugs/__snapshots__/bugreport48-569cfe.snap.svg` to lock the expected output.

### Testing
- Ran the snapshot test with `BUN_UPDATE_SNAPSHOTS=1 bun test tests/bugs/bugreport48-569cfe.test.ts`, which passed and wrote the SVG snapshot successfully. 
- Type-checked the repository with `bunx tsc --noEmit`, which completed without errors. 
- Ran the formatter with `bun run format`, which completed successfully.

------
[Codex Task](https://chatgpt.com/codex/tasks/task_b_69d4365c0ffc832e9d08e6c7297d3207)

title: Return user_note render layers for pcb_note elements
description: Returns user_note render layers for pcb_note elements, ensuring they are correctly excluded from layer-filtered renders.
body: `getElementRenderLayers` returned `[]` for `pcb_note_*` elements, treating them the same as `pcb_board` and `pcb_hole` (layer-agnostic, always drawn). This caused them to leak into copper, silkscreen, and soldermask renders when specific layers were requested. Now they return ["top_user_note", "bottom_user_note"] so layer-filtered renders correctly exclude them.

title: Trim high-density failed-node labels by removing connection list
description: Trims high-density node marker labels by removing the connection list to improve readability in visualization flows.
body:
### Motivation
- High-density node marker labels included a long `connections:` list that made failed-node/high-density visualization labels excessively large and hard to read in Pipeline4/Pipeline5 flows, so the label was trimmed for clarity.

### Description
- Removed the connection-name collection and the `connections: ...` line from `HighDensitySolver.createNodeMarkerLabel` in `lib/solvers/HighDensitySolver/HighDensitySolver.ts` and updated `tests/high-density-solver-failed-node-visualization.test.ts` to assert the label contains `portPoints` and does not contain `connections:`.

### Testing
- Ran `bun test tests/high-density-solver-failed-node-visualization.test.ts`, `bun test tests/autorouting-pipeline4-high-density-repair.test.ts`, and `bun test tests/debugger-keyboard4-port-point-download.test.ts` (all passed), and also ran `bunx tsc --noEmit` and `bun run format` successfully.

title: Add stage-by-stage PNG dumps for autorouter pipeline debug runs
description: Add stage-by-stage PNG dumps for autorouter pipeline debug runs, including a reusable PipelineStageDebugRunner for logging and PNG artifact generation during pipeline execution.
body:
## Summary
- update `graphics-debug` to `0.0.90` to use PNG rendering from `GraphicsObject`
- add a reusable `PipelineStageDebugRunner` that steps phase-based pipeline solvers and writes `logs.txt` plus per-stage PNG artifacts under `tmp/run-*`
- add `scripts/run-sample.ts` and `run-sample.sh` to run a single SRJ or benchmark sample with `--pipeline`, `--srj-path`, or `--sample`
- extract benchmark dataset/sample loading into `scripts/benchmark/scenarios.ts` so sample selection matches benchmark ordering
- add coverage for sample loading and Pipeline4 stage artifact generation

## Testing
- `bun test tests/pipeline-stage-debug-runner.test.ts tests/autorouting-pipeline4-high-density-repair.test.ts`
- `bun run build`
- `./run-sample.sh --sample 1 --pipeline 4`

title: Fix KiCad 3D models by falling back to STEP
description: Fixes 3D model rendering failure in KiCad when WRL URL returns 404 by adding a fallback to STEP models.
body: **Motivation**: When a user uses kicad-footprint a 404 on the WRL URL shouldn’t block rendering when a STEP model is available. **Fix**: KiCad footprints were failing to render in 3D because the viewer only tried the .wrl URL, which often returns 404. This change adds a safe fallback to .step so models still load when WRL is missing.

title: Update calculate-packing version to 0.0.71

body: holes were added as obstacle in the newer version, i faced this issue while creating a sparkfun board which now can be fixed after this update.

title: Wrap every schematic component in a <g> and fix primitive layering
description: Wraps every schematic component in a g element and ensures that component-owned primitives render correctly above the yellow box in SVG output.
body: Previously, `is_box_with_pins === false` components had no `<g>` wrapper, and component-owned primitives (lines, circles, rects, arcs, paths) rendered as top-level SVG elements — causing them to appear below the yellow box. Now every schematic component gets a `<g>`, and owned primitives render inside it after the `box/symbol` so they're visible on top.

title: fix: skip courtyard overlap check for different layers
description: Fixes false positives in courtyard overlap checks when components are on different PCB layers, ensuring that only same-layer overlaps trigger errors.
body:
## Summary

Fixes tscircuit/core#2084

The courtyard overlap checker was reporting false positives when two components occupy the same XY position but on different PCB layers (top vs bottom). Components on different layers don't physically interfere, so the overlap check should be skipped.

## Changes

- **`checkCourtyardOverlap.ts`**: Added layer comparison before polygon overlap check. If two courtyard elements have different `layer` values, they are skipped.
- **New test**: `courtyard-different-layers-no-overlap.test.tsx` - verifies no error for different-layer courtyards, and confirms same-layer overlap still triggers an error.

## Test plan

- [x] New test for different-layer courtyards passes
- [x] All 17 existing courtyard overlap tests still pass

title: Add working STEP cadmodel docs example with bundled SOIC-8 asset and live preview
description: Replaces the STEP cadmodel placeholder in the documentation with a complete, runnable example that includes a live preview of a 3D model using a real STEP asset.
body:
This PR replaces the STEP `cadmodel` placeholder in the docs with a complete, runnable example.

It adds a live `CircuitPreview` that demonstrates loading a STEP model into `<cadmodel />` using both:

* `modelUrl` for the hosted 3D model
* `stepFileUrl` for the local STEP asset used during STEP export

The change also includes the new `soic8.step` asset so the example is fully backed by a real file instead of placeholder text.

title: skill installation recommendation
description: Adds a new AGENTS.md file with development guidelines and updates documentation to include instructions for installing the tscircuit skill for AI assistants.

title: Update high-density-repair02: repaired traces stay inside boundaries by nudging only interior points inward
description: Fixes the issue where repaired traces extend beyond the defined boundaries by adjusting only the interior points inward.

title: change high-density-repair02 to a dev dep
description: Changes the dependency status of high-density-repair02 from a regular dependency to a development dependency in package.json

title: embedded file implementation, PcbArc
description: Adds new classes for handling embedded files and PCB arcs in the KiCad project structure, enhancing the representation of embedded files in PCB designs.

title: chore: update packages @tscircuit/circuit-json-util
description: Updates the tscircuitcircuit-json-util package from version 0.0.91 to 0.0.92 in the development dependencies.

title: Inner Layer Support
description: Adds support for additional layers in the PCB design and fixes type issues in the layer handling functions.

title: support kicad arcs
description: Adds support for handling arcs in KiCad graphics, allowing for accurate representation and processing of arc elements in PCB designs.

title: introduce site for converting kicad files to circuit json files
description: Adds a Vite-based web application for converting KiCad PCB files to Circuit JSON format, allowing users to drag-and-drop files for conversion and preview the results in-browser.

title: Separate layers when doing placement analysis
description: Separates top and bottom layer analysis in placement reports to ensure accurate component placement evaluation based on layer-specific geometries.

title: Board Utilization
description: This pull request introduces significant enhancements to the placement analysis report generation, focusing on board utilization metrics. It adds new types and functions to calculate and report on empty spaces and overall board utilization, improving the analysis capabilities of the tool.

title: Refactor CAD pipeline to preserve full model hierarchy, add material colors, and improve Three.js resource management
description: Refactors the CAD pipeline to maintain the full model hierarchy, incorporates material colors, and enhances resource management for Three.js.

title: Add CI checks and standardize formatting across CAD viewer codebase
description: This pull request introduces Continuous Integration (CI) checks for formatting and type checking in the CAD viewer codebase. It adds two GitHub Actions workflows: one for format checking and another for type checking, ensuring that code adheres to specified formatting rules and type safety. Additionally, it standardizes the formatting of existing code by removing unnecessary semicolons and ensuring consistent line endings.

title: Normalize boundary-anchored routes before repair evaluation
description: Normalizes boundary-anchored route endpoints before running the repair pipeline so routes that are effectively on a boundary are treated as exactly boundary-aligned during evaluation.
body:
Normalizes boundary-anchored route endpoints before running the repair pipeline so routes that are effectively on a boundary are treated
  as exactly boundary-aligned during evaluation.

  This fixes the cmn23 repro where a near-boundary endpoint allowed the solver to route into the boundary strip instead of rejecting the
  move. It also adds SVG snapshot coverage for that case and keeps the asset loader typed correctly for both raw samples and wrapped solver-
  input JSON.

title: Improving solver by using dynamic margin for nudgeInteriorPointsInsideBoundary
description: This pull request introduces a dynamic margin for the nudgeInteriorPointsInsideBoundary function, which aims to enhance the solvers performance by adjusting the margin dynamically based on the context of the boundary conditions. The changes include modifications to the benchmark results, increasing the sample count and adjusting various metrics to reflect the new implementation.

title: Fixing benchmark.yml
description: Fixes formatting issues in the benchmark.yml file by adding quotes around description strings for better clarity.

title: Eliminate Boundary Regressions by Clamping Interior Repair Points While Preserving DRC Clearance
description: Clamps interior repair points within boundaries to prevent new DRC clearance violations during the repair process.
body: This PR improves the reliability of the repair pipeline by ensuring interior route points are safely clamped within boundaries without introducing new DRC clearance violations. A conflict-aware validation step prevents any adjustments that would create new touches or clearance issues, resulting in more stable and higher-quality repaired routes. ## Before ## After

title: Expand boundary violation detection and standardize terminology across solver and benchmarks
description: This PR upgrades boundary handling in the high-density repair solver by broadening detection logic and aligning terminology from boundary hits to boundary violations.
body: This PR upgrades boundary handling in the high-density repair solver by broadening detection logic and aligning terminology from “boundary hits” to “boundary violations.” Key Improvements Enhanced detection logic: Boundary violations now include: Segments touching boundary edges Points outside the boundary (new behavior) This ensures more accurate identification of invalid routing cases.

title: Fix/benchmark trigger
description: Adds support for contributors in the GitHub Actions benchmark trigger workflow.

title: Handle EasyEDA undefined text tokens in single-letter shape parsing
description: Fixes parsing failure in SingleLetterShapeSchema when EasyEDA serializes missing optional text fields as the string undefined.
body:
## Summary

EasyEDA sometimes serializes missing optional text fields in `T~...` shapes as the literal string `"undefined"`, which causes `SingleLetterShapeSchema` parsing to fail before the normal defaults are applied.

This PR updates the text-shape parser to normalize those `"undefined"` fields to missing values for the optional text fields we already default or treat as optional:
- `backgroundColor`
- `fontWeight`
- `fontStyle`
- `fontDecoration`

It also adds regression coverage for that case and keeps validation strict for genuinely invalid `fontStyle` values.

## Testing

- `bun test tests/parse-tests/single-letter-shape-schema.test.ts`

title: Fix BOM export to include footprints from CAD components
description: Fixes BOM generation to include footprints from CAD components when the resolvePart does not provide it, ensuring correct export of footprints for standard tscircuit designs.
body: BOM generation was leaving the Footprint column empty unless resolvePart provided it. This change falls back to the matching cad_component.footprinter_string, so standard tscircuit designs export footprints correctly without extra manual steps.

title: Fix KiCad footprint mapping to use slash paths
description: Fixes KiCad footprint mapping to use slash paths instead of colons, ensuring compatibility with the expected kicad:LibFootprint format.
body: Motivation: Kicad footprint format is copied with colon but the eval/core expects that to be in slash(/). https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/7135a4a3-e7bf-4a98-9ff2-453ae41fd894 KiCad footprints were being mapped from .pretty / to : which breaks the expected kicad:Lib/Footprint format. This patch keeps / so imports and search results match real KiCad paths. https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f3b7d3d0-e056-4d7f-8c7a-57ff99d064e8
